raw_consumer_statement:
I just became aware of a refund that i did not received for a transaction on my card, and they won't let me file a charge back. 

In XXXX of XXXX  I prepaid for a hotel stay for later in the year. I paid extra to make sure I could cancel the reservation. My receipt from the hotel company confirmed I paid for the cancellation rate. I did cancel the reservation in time and got a reservation cancellation message from the company on XX/XX/XXXX. However, I never received the refund. In doing some end of year reporting, I can across the issue and contact them as soon as I became aware of the issue. I tried to contact the hotel, but unfortunately - even thought it was a XXXX XXXX at the time- the XXXX has declared bankruptcy. I sent all my receipts to Amex but they said there was nothing they could do. 

Travel - XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX CA {$3300.00} My card is a XXXX American Express and is also suppose to offer some protection and insurance when things like this happen. If I had known of the issue earlier, I would have dealt with it earlier. I have held this card for more than XXXX  years and Amex has so much more power than I do to go back against these companies for compensation.
